seo.title: Web Analytics tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of Web Analytics tools and references.

content.intro: A curated technical directory of web and product analytics tools designed for developers prioritizing privacy compliance, data ownership, and performance-optimized tracking.

entries.name: Plausible Analytics
entries.description: Lightweight, open-source web analytics that provides essential metrics without cookies or personal data collection.
pros: Easy integration with Next.js, Nuxt, and Gatsby
cons: Limited depth for complex product funnel analysis

entries.name: PostHog
entries.description: All-in-one product OS providing event tracking, session recording, feature flags, and A/B testing in a single library.
pros: Extensive plugin system for data pipelines
cons: High complexity for simple landing pages

entries.name: Umami
entries.description: Simple, fast, and privacy-focused alternative to Google Analytics that can be easily self-hosted on a single VPS.
cons: Manual updates required for self-hosted instances

entries.name: Fathom Analytics
entries.description: Privacy-first SaaS analytics focused on performance and compliance for high-traffic websites.
pros: Custom domains for tracking to bypass ad-blockers

entries.name: Matomo
entries.description: Feature-rich analytics platform offering 100% data ownership and a direct alternative to GA4's reporting depth.
pros: Import tools for legacy Google Analytics data
cons: Heavier script footprint compared to Plausible
UI can feel dated and complex

entries.name: Mixpanel
entries.description: Event-based analytics platform designed for deep analysis of user journeys and retention patterns.
cons: Steep learning curve for non-technical users
Costs scale rapidly with high event volumes

entries.name: Pirsch Analytics
entries.description: Server-side and client-side analytics focused on being lightweight and developer-friendly via a robust API.
pros: Server-side tracking avoids all client-side blocks

entries.name: Simple Analytics
entries.description: Clean, simple analytics that never collects personal data or uses cookies, with a focus on simplicity.

entries.name: Amplitude
entries.description: Enterprise-grade product analytics specializing in behavioral data and user lifecycle management.
cons: Enterprise pricing is opaque and expensive

entries.name: Ackee
entries.description: Node.js based analytics tool for developers who want a private, self-hosted GraphQL API for their data.
pros: Native GraphQL API for custom frontends
